metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the OFFLINE test produces a non-empty `recommend_overlays` from the base step (e.g., extend FakeAgentRunner for `mode='structured'`, inject a stub step output, or record a canned replay), so the AC's Round-B membership assertion is actually reachable offline.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that an OFFLINE (FakeRunner) test will exercise the full two-round topology — including Round-B membership driven by `recommend_overlays` from the base step — but `FakeAgentRunner.run()` in `workflow/runners.py:44-54` returns `{'result': {}, '_fake': True}` for non-findings/non-text modes, and for `mode='findings'` returns `{findings: [], summary: '...'}` with NO `recommend_overlays` key. The plan does not explain how the FakeRunner is extended or replaced to produce the `recommend_overlays` output the `overlay_union` step consumes offline, leaving the capability assertion unverified.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — '[ ] An OFFLINE (FakeRunner) test asserts: Round-B membership == the escalated set'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before authoring the AC3 offline test, extend `FakeAgentRunner` (or introduce a test-local subclass) to support a `mode='structured_overlay'` (or equivalent) that emits `{findings: [...], recommend_overlays: [...]}`, so the overlay_union step receives a non-empty recommend_overlays and the escalation wiring is genuinely exercised offline.
findings.finding: The offline (FakeAgentRunner) test required by AC3 depends on the base agent step producing both `findings` AND `recommend_overlays` as separately-referenceable named outputs, but FakeAgentRunner (runners.py:44-54) has no mode that produces this dual-output shape — the `else` branch for unknown modes returns `{result: {}, _fake: True}`, so overlay_union would receive no recommend_overlays and the AC would silently pass with an empty escalation set rather than exercising real escalation wiring.
findings.suggested_fix: In FakeAgentRunner.run(), add: `elif mode == 'structured_overlay': outputs = {'findings': [], 'recommend_overlays': [], 'summary': f'[fake] {ctx.step_id}', '_fake': True}` — or define a PlannedTraceRunner override in the test that injects a canned base-step output with a non-empty recommend_overlays list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a scope item to patch FakeAgentRunner (or provide a code-review-specific fake stub) for mode='structured' so it returns a well-formed `{findings: [], recommend_overlays: [...]}` shape; specify the stub's output contract so the AC3 offline test is non-vacuous.
findings.finding: The `FakeAgentRunner` (runners.py:44-54) returns `{result: {}, _fake: True}` for any mode other than 'findings' or 'text' — it does NOT produce `{findings: [], recommend_overlays: [...]}` for the base step which runs as `mode='structured'`. The plan's AC3 requires an OFFLINE (FakeRunner) test that 'Round-B membership == escalated set', which depends on the fake base step returning a well-formed recommend_overlays list. No fix to FakeAgentRunner is mentioned in this plan.
findings.location: AC3 — 'An OFFLINE (FakeRunner) test asserts: Round-B membership == the escalated set'; src/rebar/llm/workflow/runners.py:44-54
findings.suggested_fix: In runners.py, extend the mode=='structured' branch to return `{findings: [], recommend_overlays: [], _fake: True}` as the base case, or add a named `FakeCodeReviewBaseRunner` that the offline test injects with a configurable recommend_overlays list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'glob' precisely in the plan: state whether it is the full registered-overlay enumeration, the diff-triggered subset, or a filesystem glob pattern, and name the runtime source from which `overlay_union` reads it.
findings.finding: The term 'glob' in the union formula '(glob U base.recommend) - already-run' is never defined in the plan. It is used as a set operand but could mean: all registered overlays, a filesystem glob pattern, or the diff-triggered overlay set. An agent implementing `overlay_union` cannot write the correct set computation without this definition.
findings.location: ## What — overlay_union description; AC line 'overlay_union computes (glob U recommend) - already-run'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'glob' with an explicit label, e.g. 'triggered_overlays (the set emitted by the overlay_triggers step)' or 'all_registered_overlays (the enum of valid overlay ids)' and update both the What description and the AC line accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'glob' precisely in the plan: state whether it is the full registered-overlay enumeration (OVERLAY_IDS), the diff-triggered subset from overlay_triggers output, or another source, and name the runtime artifact overlay_union reads it from.
findings.finding: The 'glob' operand in the union formula '(glob U base.recommend) - already-run' is never defined. It appears as a named set in both the What section and AC1, but no section of the plan states what 'glob' contains — whether it means all registered overlays (the full OVERLAY_IDS enum), the diff-triggered subset (output of the overlay_triggers step), or a filesystem glob match. An implementer of overlay_union cannot write the correct set computation without this definition.
findings.location: ## What — overlay_union description; AC line 'overlay_union computes (glob U recommend) - already-run, ONE-HOP, capped at N=3 (configurable), enum-constrained'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'glob' with 'triggered_overlays (the set of overlay IDs emitted as true by the overlay_triggers step)' or 'all_registered_overlays (OVERLAY_IDS)' in both the What description and the AC line, and update the overlay_union with: block to name the input key explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `merge_findings` is a thin `uses:` adapter over the existing `aggregate_findings(per_reviewer)` (preferred — zero new cluster logic) or justify concretely why a new implementation is needed; update the plan's Scope/Specifics section to name the approach explicitly.
findings.finding: The plan introduces `merge_findings` as a new scripted op that 'clusters near-duplicate findings (reuse aggregate.py clustering by file-anchor+dimension+line-proximity) BEFORE Pass-2', but `aggregate.py:aggregate_findings()` already does exactly this — it accepts a list of per-reviewer result dicts or (reviewer_id, findings) pairs, clusters by `_file_anchor` + dimension + `_LINE_BUCKET` line-proximity, de-duplicates, and ranks. The existing `code_review.py` already calls it for the identical merging purpose. The plan should justify why a thin `uses:` step wrapping `aggregate_findings([base_result, round_a_result, round_b_result])` cannot serve, rather than authoring a new op that reinvents proven code.
findings.location: ## What / ## Specifics pinned / Added AC: '`merge_findings` dedups/clusters near-duplicate findings'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the `merge_findings` op description with: '`merge_findings` is a thin scripted-step adapter that calls `aggregate.aggregate_findings([base_result, round_a_result, round_b_result])` directly — no new cluster/dedup logic, provenance (reviewers field) is preserved by the existing function.' This matches the existing pattern in `code_review.py` and requires ~5 lines instead of ~30.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the `overlay_union` implementation spec that `recommend_overlays` is read from exactly one explicit `with:` input wired to the **base** step's output, and that the op ignores any inputs that would come from Round-B steps — so the one-hop guarantee is enforced by data-flow construction, not only by test assertion.
findings.finding: The one-hop enforcement mechanism is under-specified: the plan asserts the invariant ('overlays do not re-escalate') and plans a test for it, but does not specify HOW `overlay_union` is constrained to read `recommend_overlays` ONLY from the base step's output and never from Round-B agent outputs. If Round-B batch agent steps also emit `recommend_overlays`, a future `overlay_union` call (or a poorly-wired YAML) could silently consume them and break the one-hop guarantee. The plan needs to state an implementation-level enforcement: `overlay_union` reads only a named input wired to the base step's output — not any downstream step's outputs — and the `code-review.yaml` wiring should make this explicit.
findings.location: ## Specifics pinned / AC: 'one-hop holds (overlays do not re-escalate)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the `overlay_union` op signature, accept `base_recommend: list[str]` (not a generic `recommend_overlays`) as a named input, and document that `code-review.yaml` wires it exclusively to `steps.base.outputs.recommend_overlays`. The linter's static expression checker will then catch any wiring to a Round-B step at workflow-validate time.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the ADR file path (e.g. `docs/adr/NNN-overlay-escalation.md`) to the Scope section so the deliverable list is complete and consistent with the Added ACs.
findings.finding: The Added ACs require an ADR document to be produced, but the Scope section's file list does not include any ADR path. This means the deliverable set in Scope and the deliverable set in Added ACs are inconsistent — an agent tracking only the Scope file list would not produce the ADR.
findings.location: Scope file list vs Added ACs — 'ADR records the one-hop base→overlay escalation design'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `docs/adr/NNN-overlay-escalation.md` (or the project's conventional ADR path) as a third Scope bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in Added AC3 (and the body) WHERE the config schema is documented — e.g., a `## Configuration` section in `gates/code-review.yaml` header comments, a `docs/gates/code-review-config.md` page, or a JSON Schema file — so the AC is verifiable.
findings.finding: Added AC3 requires the `gates/code-review.yaml` config schema to be 'documented' but specifies no location, format, or artifact — the body says 'DOCUMENTED schema (fields/types/defaults/valid ranges)' without naming where (inline YAML comments, a separate doc page, a README section).
findings.location: Plan-review remediation / Added ACs — 'gates/code-review.yaml config schema documented (fields/types/defaults/ranges)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the remediation body: 'The schema is documented as a `## Configuration` block at the top of `gates/code-review.yaml` (YAML comments) AND referenced from the ADR.' Update Added AC3 to: 'gates/code-review.yaml contains a structured ## Configuration header block listing all operator fields with types, defaults, and valid ranges.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback for overlay_union when already_run is absent or empty: either document that it defaults to the empty set (and update the config schema AC to include this default) or specify that Round-A always emits a non-null already_run key.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no fallback for overlay_union when the explicitly-required `already_run` input is absent or empty (e.g. when Round-A produces no batch output — a zero-criteria trigger set). The processing rule 'treat missing already_run as empty set' vs 'raise ValueError' is unspecified, and the config-schema AC ('documented fields/types/defaults/ranges') does not call out a default for this input.
findings.location: ## Specifics pinned — 'overlay_union receives already_run EXPLICITLY via its with: block'; Added AC — 'overlay_union reads already_run from an explicit with: input'; gates/code-review.yaml config schema AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the config-schema documentation: 'already_run: list[str], default []' and specify in the overlay_union description that a missing/null already_run is treated as the empty set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decide and pin the single module location for `overlay_union` and `merge_findings` (remove the 'or' alternative) so the YAML step references a deterministic import path.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists the implementation file as '`src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py` (or register in `workflow/steps.py`)' — the 'or' leaves the module location undecided, a real design choice deferred to the executor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in the 'Why' or 'What' section explaining that two rounds are necessary because the base reviewer's `recommend_overlays` output is not available until after Round-A completes — a single-round approach cannot use base findings to drive overlay selection, which is the core recall improvement this WS delivers.
findings.finding: The plan lacks a positive rationale for the chosen two-round topology over a structurally simpler single-round alternative (running all overlays in one batch with a combined glob-OR-recommend trigger), leaving the architectural choice undocumented. The 'Why' section describes the integration goal but not why two rounds were selected over the alternatives.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the line-proximity window (default value and valid range) used by `merge_findings` clustering, either as a hard-coded default or as a named config field in the `gates/code-review.yaml` schema.
findings.finding: The `merge_findings` clustering criterion references 'line-proximity' as a clustering dimension but leaves the proximity threshold entirely unstated. An implementer must choose a window (e.g. ±5 lines, ±10 lines) with no guidance, and the AC ('N overlays on one location collapse') cannot be written as a deterministic test without a fixed threshold.
findings.location: ## Plan-review remediation — '`merge_findings` CLUSTERS near-duplicate findings (reuse `aggregate.py` clustering by file-anchor + dimension + line-proximity)'; Added AC: 'test asserts N overlays on one location collapse'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC4's kernel function names (`verify_findings`, `pass3_over_findings`, `coach`) with the body's labels ('Pass-2/3/4', 'coach_gate/coach') — either update the body to introduce these names or update AC4 to use the same labels the body uses.
findings.finding: AC4 ('The gate calls the kernel verify_findings / pass3_over_findings / coach directly') references specific function-level names that never appear in the plan body, which only refers to 'kernel Pass-2/3/4' and 'coach_gate/coach (Pass-4)'. If those identifiers differ from the actual kernel API, the AC is unverifiable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 ('The gate calls the kernel verify_findings / pass3_over_findings / coach directly (no forked passes).')
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan body (## What or ## Specifics), add a one-line mapping: 'kernel entry points: verify_findings (Pass-2), pass3_over_findings (Pass-3), coach (Pass-4)' so AC4's identifiers are grounded.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the overlay ID strings (or cite a concrete WS2 deliverable by file path and constant name) in this plan so the overlay_union output schema and the 'enum-constrained' AC can be implemented without referencing an undelivered artifact.
findings.finding: The `include_<overlay>` output flags and `to_run` set emitted by overlay_union have an undefined key vocabulary: the overlay IDs that form the flag names are bound to the `OVERLAY_IDS` enum from WS2/registry.py, but that enum's member strings are never enumerated in this plan. An executor writing overlay_union cannot know which flag names to emit without cross-referencing an undelivered WS2 artifact.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
